environment.objective: Build the strongest fixed-host benchmark harness for canonical, production-ready native-language implementations, with correctness enforced before any ranking.
environment.scoring_balance: equal category weight, benchmark weights normalized within category
environment.link_policy: toolchain-default release mode (mixed linkage; see entry metadata)

entries.label: c (gcc)
optimization_notes: O3 plus LTO for whole-program release builds
native CPU tuning
frame-pointer omission and low-cost math errno cleanup
lld when available, otherwise toolchain default linker

entries.label: c (clang)
optimization_notes: O3 plus LTO for whole-program release builds
native CPU tuning
frame-pointer omission and low-cost math errno cleanup
lld when available, otherwise toolchain default linker

entries.label: go (gc)
optimization_notes: optimized default Go compiler pipeline
trimpath and buildvcs disabled for cleaner reproducible artifacts
linker stripping and empty buildid for lean release binaries

entries.label: moonbit (native)
optimization_notes: native target release build
toolchain-managed stripping
frozen dependency graph for reproducible builds

entries.label: nim (gcc)
optimization_notes: danger mode plus speed optimization
ORC memory manager
C compiler native tuning with LTO
lld when available, otherwise toolchain default linker

entries.label: nim (clang)
optimization_notes: danger mode plus speed optimization
ORC memory manager
C compiler native tuning with LTO
lld when available, otherwise toolchain default linker

entries.label: ocaml (native)
optimization_notes: native-code release build with unsafe and nodynlink
C backend native tuning flags passed through ccopt
separate stripping step after build

entries.label: rust (rustc/llvm)
optimization_notes: target-cpu=native
thin LTO and single codegen unit
panic abort and symbol stripping for release binaries

entries.label: sarif (stage0/native)
optimization_notes: native executable emitted through sarifc build
stdout result mode for benchmark output parity
retained benchmark inputs declared in per-benchmark specs

benchmarks.name: binarytrees
benchmarks.algorithm: bottom-up binary tree construction and checksum
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Same tree/check workload; memory-management costs remain language-native.
benchmarks.retained_for: Only benchmark centered on allocation-heavy pointer traversal and recursive tree construction.

benchmarks.name: csvgroupby
benchmarks.algorithm: CSV parse plus per-customer group-by aggregation
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Uses a committed deterministic CSV fixture with clean unquoted fields and sorted aggregate output.
benchmarks.retained_for: Anchors real structured-text parsing plus aggregation with lighter state than the join workload.

benchmarks.name: joinagg
benchmarks.algorithm: sectioned CSV parse plus active-user sort-merge join and ordered aggregation
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Uses one deterministic two-section fixture, keeps the join key and aggregate contract fixed, and adds a realistic relational join workload without external libraries.
benchmarks.retained_for: Only retained workload that exercises join logic, multi-table data shaping, and ordered aggregation together.

benchmarks.name: fasta
benchmarks.algorithm: deterministic FASTA generation with buffered text emission
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Adds text generation and formatting without turning the suite into a library benchmark.
benchmarks.retained_for: Covers deterministic text generation and sustained buffered output, which parsing workloads do not.

benchmarks.name: mandelbrot
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Input size is set to 512 because all retained implementations agree there exactly.
benchmarks.retained_for: Represents scalar numeric compute with tight loop and branch behavior distinct from floating-point iterative kernels.

benchmarks.name: spectralnorm
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Correctness compares canonical 9-decimal output, not raw printer differences.
benchmarks.retained_for: Adds floating-point iterative linear-algebra style work with stable numeric comparison rules.

benchmarks.name: nbody
benchmarks.retained_for: Only retained simulation-style kernel, preserving long-running floating-point update behavior.

benchmarks.name: knucleotide
benchmarks.algorithm: FASTA parsing plus k-mer frequency and occurrence counting
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Uses one committed deterministic FASTA fixture and processes only the >THREE section.
benchmarks.retained_for: Primary hash-heavy and string-heavy benchmark; no other workload stresses this mix as directly.

benchmarks.name: revcomp
benchmarks.algorithm: FASTA parsing plus reverse-complement text transformation
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Adds streaming-style text transformation and output reshaping with the same committed fixture family.
benchmarks.retained_for: Keeps a transformation-oriented streaming workload in the suite instead of only generators and aggregators.

benchmarks.name: sortuniq
benchmarks.algorithm: line-oriented string sort and duplicate-count aggregation
benchmarks.fairness_notes: Uses one committed deterministic newline-word fixture, includes empty-line noise, and rewards lean sort-plus-aggregation implementations.
benchmarks.retained_for: Captures global ordering cost and frequency aggregation without parser-heavy setup or join complexity.

decision_profiles.label: Balanced
decision_profiles.description: Default composite across speed, memory, build time, and binary size.

decision_profiles.label: Build First
decision_profiles.description: Build and iteration cost matter most.
